sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a chronic history of back pain, which has worsened over the past month.
The patient reports difficulty standing, sitting, and walking due to pain, and describes the pain as an electrical toothache that radiates from the lower back down to the left leg.
The pain is exacerbated by coughing, sneezing, and standing, and is relieved by lying down.
The patient rates the pain as 6 out of 10 currently, but states it reaches 13 out of 10 when severe.
The patient also reports weakness in both legs and numbness in the lower extremities.
The patient's pain has been persistent for 30 years, dating back to a fall from a roof, and has been exacerbated by a possible fracture or disk herniation.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of a possible fracture or disk herniation from a fall from a roof 30 years ago.
The patient has also tried NSAIDs and physical therapy (PT) in the past without relief.
Review of systems: The patient reports pain and weakness in the lower back, hip, leg, and calf, with numbness and tingling in the lower extremities.
Current medications: NOT MENTIONED
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has positive pain to palpation at the L4 vertebrae, and pain with flexion and extension of the back.
The patient has 4 out of 5 strength in the left leg and 5 out of 5 strength in the right leg.
Reflexes are brisk and motor and sensory are intact in both lower extremities.
Test Results: The MRI shows a disk herniation at the level of L4-L5 vertebrae, associated with nerve root impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement, causing lower back pain and pain down the left leg.
Problem list: Acute disk herniation at L4-L5; nerve root impingement; lower back pain; left leg pain.
Plan:
I prescribed an epidural steroid injection to reduce inflammation and relieve pain.
I will refer the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the area.
The patient will see me again in about a month to follow up on the treatment.
Follow-up: The patient will see me again in about a month.
